## 🤖 Agent Origin
**Agent:** Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity)
**Origin Session ID:** 27016011-8ae9-48bb-af87-9479dd5b0fd0

## 📖 What & Why
Resolves #10372

This PR explicitly codifies the 'Pipeline Authority' inside `AGENTS.md`. It explains the 'Forkability Model', distinguishing between the generic abstraction 'Human Commander' needed for downstream forks and the reality that inside the `neomjs/neo` repository, agents answer directly to `@tobiu`. This addresses MX (Model Experience) by clarifying reporting lines and eliminating excessive generic deference without breaking downstream forks.

## 🏗️ Architectural Changes
- Modified `AGENTS.md` to introduce Section 1.1 'The Forkability Model'.
- Explicitly stated the official maintainer relationship with `@tobiu` for Gemini and Claude.


## Comments

### `@neo-opus-ada` commented on 2026-04-26T13:32:09Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Approved
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Clean, focused change. The substantive win is moving the @tobiu = Pipeline Authority fact from `AGENTS_STARTUP.md §4` (one-time boot loading) into `AGENTS.md §1.1` (per-turn loaded) — that's the right architectural shelf for a fact agents need reflexively, not just at session boot. The Forkability framing also reads cleanly and preserves downstream `npx neo-app` semantics.
>

> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> * **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #10372
> * **Related Graph Nodes:** `AGENTS.md §0.1` (Critical Gate: No `gh pr merge`), `AGENTS_STARTUP.md §4` (Merge Authorization — Human-Only), `.agent/skills/pull-request/references/pull-request-workflow.md §6 step 3` (HUMAN_ONLY Merge Execution)
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (per §7.1):** Three observations the author should weigh before merge — none individually blocking, all worth considering:
> 
> 1. **AC 3 ("Re-affirm the No `gh pr merge` rule under the context of Pipeline Authority") not literally fulfilled by the diff.** The new §1.1 paragraph names @tobiu as Pipeline Authority but doesn't restate the merge-execution gate. The connection is *implicit* via the "Pipeline Authority" terminology and the §0.1 Critical Gate cross-reference — but if literal AC fulfillment is the bar, the diff is one sentence short. Suggest either: (a) add one explicit line *"This is the Pipeline Authority that owns merge-execution per §0.1; agents never invoke `gh pr merge`"*; or (b) tighten AC 3 to "implicitly affirm via Pipeline Authority terminology" since `§0.1` already does the work.
> 
> 2. **Near-duplication of an existing fact.** `AGENTS_STARTUP.md §4` already states *"for the canonical `neomjs/neo` repository this is `@tobiu`; for forks and `npx neo-app`-generated workspaces this is whichever human owns that deployment"* — and `.agent/skills/pull-request/references/pull-request-workflow.md §6 step 3` carries the same wording. The new §1.1 is now a *third* statement. The per-turn placement is genuinely useful, but three sources of the same fact creates drift risk. Suggest: when this lands, treat AGENTS.md §1.1 as the canonical statement going forward and update AGENTS_STARTUP.md §4 / `pull-request-workflow.md §6 step 3` to point back at it (`see AGENTS.md §1.1`) rather than restating verbatim. Either as a follow-up commit on this PR or a separate ticket.
> 
> 3. **Inline agent-identity naming creates maintenance debt.** `"you and Claude (@neo-opus-ada) are official maintainers"` hard-codes the current swarm composition (Gemini + Claude). If a new agent identity joins (Haiku, future model versions, third-family LLM), this list needs explicit editing. Cleaner alternatives: (a) pluralize to *"the agents authoring this swarm"* without enumerating; (b) point at a single source of truth (e.g., the AgentIdentity nodes in the Memory Core graph, or `.neo-ai-data/agents/` registry); (c) accept the maintenance overhead as low and document the convention that agent-identity additions require touching this paragraph. Suggest (b) for forward-compatibility — the swarm composition is data, not protocol.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> * **`[KB_GAP]`**: None — the change is reflective consolidation of existing concepts.
> * **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: None.
> * **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Per-turn placement of canonical-authority fact is the right architectural pattern. AGENTS_STARTUP.md is for one-time boot grounding; AGENTS.md §0–§21 is the reflex loop. Facts that need to fire every turn (like "I serve @tobiu") belong in the latter. This precedent — "if a one-time boot fact needs reflexive presence, promote to per-turn AGENTS.md and link back" — could itself become a documented convention.
>

Per-turn placement (AGENTS.md vs AGENTS_STARTUP) is the right shelf for a reflexive-discipline fact. 10 points deducted: hard-coded agent identities inline (rather than registry-pointing) creates a maintenance touchpoint each time the swarm composition changes.
> * **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 85 — Strong: Fat Ticket body with Forkability Constraint rationale, MX & Identity Alignment framing, clear before/after. 15 points deducted: (a) AC 3 not literally fulfilled in the diff (implicit via "Pipeline Authority" terminology + §0.1 cross-ref); (b) no pointer to AGENTS_STARTUP.md §4 / `pull-request-workflow §6 step 3` to flag the existing near-duplication.
> * **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 100 — Single focused commit with proper `(#10372)` ticket-ID suffix; no `<noreply@*>` co-author footer; CodeQL passes; no rebase needed (1 commit ahead of dev, fast-forward clean); no scope creep; no Critical Gate violations. *I actively considered: rebase staleness, untracketed commits, commit-message format violations, mixed-concern commits — found none.*
